---
title: "4.15 Sorting Algorithms"
subtitle: "Data Collections"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### Sorting puts data in order

<span class="term">Sorting algorithms</span> reorder data, usually from smallest to largest or alphabetically.

This topic focuses on:

::: {.tight-list}
- selection sort
- insertion sort
- tracing how an array changes
- informal runtime comparison
:::

## Selection Sort Idea

### Repeatedly select the smallest remaining value

Algorithm:

```text
For each position from left to right:
    Find the smallest value in the unsorted part.
    Swap it into the current position.
```

After each pass, one more value is in its final position.

## Selection Sort Code {.fit-small}

### Find min index, then swap

```java
for (int i = 0; i < values.length - 1; i++)
{
    int minIndex = i;
    for (int j = i + 1; j < values.length; j++)
    {
        if (values[j] < values[minIndex])
        {
            minIndex = j;
        }
    }
    int temp = values[i];
    values[i] = values[minIndex];
    values[minIndex] = temp;
}
```

## Selection Sort Trace

### First pass

For `{5, 2, 7, 1}`:

::: {.tight-list}
- current position: index `0`
- smallest remaining value: `1` at index `3`
- swap indices `0` and `3`
- array becomes `{1, 2, 7, 5}`
:::

## Insertion Sort Idea

### Insert each value into the sorted left side

Algorithm:

```text
Start with the left side considered sorted.
Take the next value.
Shift larger values right.
Insert the value into the open spot.
```

Insertion sort resembles sorting cards in your hand.

## Insertion Sort Code {.fit-small}

### Shift until the correct position opens

```java
for (int i = 1; i < values.length; i++)
{
    int current = values[i];
    int j = i - 1;

    while (j >= 0 && values[j] > current)
    {
        values[j + 1] = values[j];
        j--;
    }
    values[j + 1] = current;
}
```

## Insertion Sort Trace

### Insert `2`

For `{5, 2, 7, 1}` at `i = 1`:

::: {.tight-list}
- `current = 2`
- compare with `5`
- shift `5` right
- insert `2` at index `0`
- array becomes `{2, 5, 7, 1}`
:::

## Groupwork Coding Challenge

### Sort runtimes

Trace selection sort and insertion sort on the same small array.

Record:

::: {.tight-list}
- comparisons
- swaps or shifts
- array after each outer loop pass
- which algorithm feels easier to trace and why
:::

## Classroom Check

### A strong answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- explain selection sort as choosing the minimum remaining value
- trace a selection sort swap
- explain insertion sort as inserting into a sorted left side
- trace an insertion sort shift
- compare sorting algorithms using passes, comparisons, swaps, and shifts
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
